documents.text: PostgreSQL 13 to 15 upgrade via pg_upgrade is supported but requires careful planning. Breaking changes include: removal of old replication slot behavior for logical replication, stricter SCRAM-SHA-256 authentication defaults (md5 auth deprecated), adminpack extension removed from contrib, and changes to JSON path query functions. Applications using psycopg2 2.8 or older may fail SSL handshake with PG15. SQLAlchemy 1.3 is not fully compatible with PG15 — upgrade to SQLAlchemy 1.4+ or 2.0. Review all custom PL/pgSQL functions using deprecated JSON operators (-> vs ->>).

PostgreSQL configuration risks during major version upgrades: wal_level, max_connections, shared_buffers, and autovacuum settings often need retuning after upgrade. Replication lag can spike during cutover if standby nodes are not pre-synced. Extensions such as pg_stat_statements, PostGIS, and pg_cron must be upgraded separately using ALTER EXTENSION. pg_hba.conf authentication method changes can block application connections silently. Always run pg_upgrade --check before actual migration and validate backup/restore in staging.

psycopg2 and psycopg2-binary compatibility with PostgreSQL 15: psycopg2 >= 2.9.3 recommended for PG15 SSL and SCRAM support. Older psycopg2 2.7/2.8 builds may raise OperationalError on SCRAM authentication. psycopg3 (psycopg package) is the modern replacement with async support. SQLAlchemy connection strings using sslmode=require must be validated after PG15 upgrade. Connection pool settings (pool_size, max_overflow) may need adjustment due to changed default timeouts.

PostgreSQL integration risks for ETL and BI layers: JDBC drivers (PostgreSQL JDBC 42.x) must be updated for PG15 compatibility. Debezium CDC connectors require version 2.x for PG15 logical replication. dbt-postgres adapter versions below 1.5 may generate incompatible SQL. AWS DMS tasks need engine version mapping updated. BI tools (Tableau, Power BI, Metabase) using native PostgreSQL connectors should be tested for query plan changes on upgraded statistics.

PostgreSQL 14 to 16 upgrade notes: PG16 introduces logical decoding on standby replicas, changes to vacuum freezing behavior, and removal of some deprecated recovery settings. Query planner statistics changes may cause performance regressions on complex joins. pg_stat_statements normalized query text format changed. Monitor for increased WAL generation during first vacuum cycle post-upgrade.
